title: Logical-move type
description: Configure type = LOGICAL_MOVE for non-worker logical routing and guarded loop-breaker usage through a HOSTED_WORKER companion.
openingSummary: LOGICAL_MOVE is the WorkstationType for non-worker logical routing with optional guards as loop breakers. Set type to LOGICAL_MOVE when a step must route work without agent, model, inference, script, or poller runtimes, and choose behavior independently for STANDARD, REPEATER, CRON, or POLLER scheduling. Keep classificationRoutes on CLASSIFIER_WORKSTATION instead.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Author a workstation with type LOGICAL_MOVE and bind a HOSTED_WORKER companion. Choose behavior for the scheduling axis you need — STANDARD for fire-once routing, REPEATER for change-triggered loops that need a guard exit, CRON for schedules, or POLLER for long-lived poller ingress. Wire inputs plus outputs, onContinue, onRejection, and onFailure so logical outcomes route correctly. Set guards when this step must block or break a loop before continuing. Do not set classificationRoutes, openCodeAgent, promptFile, outcomeFormat, operation, operationBindings, or cron fields that belong to other WorkstationType or WorkstationKind families. Choose CLASSIFIER_WORKSTATION for classification routes, AGENT_RUN for agent loops, or MODEL_INVOKE for provider-neutral model operations. LOGICAL_MOVE is not a scheduling behavior and is not CLASSIFIER_WORKSTATION — keep classificationRoutes on the classifier type. Both share HOSTED_WORKER companions, but their selected field sets stay separate.

schemaReference.title: Schema reference
schemaReference.body: The embed below shows the live Workstation base definition with LOGICAL_MOVE overlay applicability. LOGICAL_MOVE selects the exclusive guards field. Excluded fields belong to other WorkstationType families or to CRON scheduling and must not appear on this type. Open the full Factory schema reference for exhaustive field lookup rather than copying contract text into this page.

examples.title: Examples
examples.body: Use the minimal valid example when starting a LOGICAL_MOVE with behavior STANDARD and optional guards. The misuse example borrows classificationRoutes from CLASSIFIER_WORKSTATION — that field is excluded from LOGICAL_MOVE.

links.companionIntro: LOGICAL_MOVE requires a HOSTED_WORKER companion and is compatible with every published WorkstationKind. Bind the hosted worker for logical routing, then choose scheduling on the behavior axis. CLASSIFIER_WORKSTATION also uses HOSTED_WORKER but selects classificationRoutes instead of guards.
links.misuseNote: classificationRoutes belongs on CLASSIFIER_WORKSTATION. Keep LOGICAL_MOVE on identity, worker binding, inputs, outputs, routing fields, and optional guards only.
